summary: Matched five previously stubbed game-mode/menu functions at 100%: mn_802308F0, fn_801A7FB4, fn_801A9FCC, fn_80165E7C, and fn_8016F870. The PR was split from #2345 and focused on game-loop and menu matches only, adding concrete C implementations plus tighter prototypes, one menu struct layout refinement, static data for the opening performance overlay, and required baselib/library includes.
what_changed: Implemented fn_80165E7C in gm_1601.c as a team-standings aggregator over six player slots, skipping slot_type 3 and accumulating score, subscore, and active state into team_standings.
Updated fn_80165E7C prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void fn_80165E7C(MatchEnd*).
Implemented fn_8016F870 in gm_16F1.c as a reverse bonus lookup returning the highest eligible bonus index below arg1, with bounds checks for arg1 == 0 or arg1 > 0x101.
Updated fn_8016F870 prototype to int fn_8016F870(void*, u16, u8, u8).
Implemented fn_801A7FB4 in gm_1A4C.c as a fog animation gate using GET_FOG, HSD_FogInterpretAnim, gm_801A659C, and un_803048C0 count thresholds.
Added <sysdolphin/baselib/fog.h> for HSD_Fog/GET_FOG usage and updated fn_801A7FB4 prototype to void fn_801A7FB4(HSD_GObj*).

Implemented fn_801A9FCC in gmopening.c to build a static linked list of PerfLabelLine entries containing opening/performance metric strings from lbMthp helpers and a CPU tick conversion using address 0x800000F8.
Added static data gm_803DBF78 containing float data and color-tagged format strings, plus static PerfLabelLine gm_80480B38[4].
Changed fn_801AA0E8 to cast fn_801A9FCC to Event when passing it to hsd_80392528.

Implemented mn_802308F0 in mnmainrule.c as a rule-description text manager callback that creates or destroys HSD_Text based on data->xA mode, current selection, hovered selection, confirmed selection, arg1, and arg2.
Refined struct mn_802307F8_t in mnmainrule.h by replacing a single opaque pad_3 array with explicit x3_pad, xA mode byte, xB_pad, and text at offset 0x130.
Updated mn_802308F0 prototype to void mn_802308F0(HSD_GObj*, int, int).
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrowly scoped to five 100% matches split from a larger pending-matches branch, reducing review surface and making progress easy to verify.
Converted unknown prototypes to typed signatures in matching headers at the same time as adding implementations, improving downstream call-site type safety.
Preserved exact-looking control-flow artifacts needed for matching, including goto labels in fn_8016F870 and PAD_STACK uses in fn_8016F870/fn_801A7FB4.
Modeled mn_802307F8_t only as far as needed for the match, exposing xA and text while retaining padding for unknown regions rather than overclaiming field names.
Used existing engine/library APIs rather than inventing abstractions: HSD_GObjGetUserData, HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4, GET_FOG, HSD_FogInterpretAnim, lbMthp_8001F5F4/E4/D4, and hsd_80392528.
For fn_8016F870, preserved the sentinel-based table scan over lbl_803D5A4C and split generic versus special bonus checks through pl_80039418 and fn_801701C0.
decomp_lessons: For 100% matches, it can be better to expose one newly understood struct byte and leave surrounding regions padded than to prematurely name an entire structure.
Reverse iteration and sentinel-table scans are important semantic and matching details; fn_8016F870 searches from arg1 - 1 downward and stops table searches at kind 0x29A.
Typed headers should be updated alongside matched bodies so future decomp work can reuse concrete signatures rather than propagating UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Game UI callbacks may read raw object user data and branch on small mode bytes; documenting offsets such as xA and text at 0x130 makes later menu work easier.
Static local/global data layout can be required for matching display/overlay code; gmopening.c added a mixed float/string struct and static PerfLabelLine array rather than inlining all constants.
Hardware-address reads can appear in matched code; fn_801A9FCC uses 0x800000F8 for bus-clock-derived CPU tick to millisecond conversion.
Some apparently redundant calls or ternaries may be matching-sensitive, such as repeated gm_801A4310() calls and un_803048C0(...) ? true : false in fn_801A7FB4.
Function-pointer APIs may require explicit casts once callback return types are made concrete, as with casting fn_801A9FCC to Event for hsd_80392528.
naming_conventions: Address-based unknown function names were retained for unmatched/unnamed functions: fn_80165E7C, fn_8016F870, fn_801A7FB4, fn_801A9FCC, mn_802308F0.
Game-mode files use gm_ address prefixes for known globals/functions, such as gm_801A4310, gm_801A659C, gm_803DBF78, and gm_80480B38.
Menu functions use mn_ prefixes and address suffixes, as in mn_802307F8, mn_802308F0, and mn_804A04F0.
Partially understood struct fields retain offset-style names such as xA, x1, x30, unk_04, x3_pad, and xB_pad.
Header comments preserve function address markers, for example /* 165E7C */, /* 16F870 */, /* 1A7FB4 */, /* 1A9FCC */, and /* 2308F0 */.
Static data names follow project address-label style when semantic names are not yet known, such as gm_803DBF78 and gm_80480B38.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) after fn_8016F870 return and PAD_STACK(0x10) in fn_801A7FB4, indicating stack-shape adjustments for exact matching.
Used goto labels check1/check2 in fn_8016F870 to share post-scan mask-check paths after sentinel fallback, likely matching original branch structure.
Kept explicit casts such as (u8) mask, (unsigned) fn_801701C0(...), (u8) arg0->player_standings[i].slot_type, and u8* data = (u8*) &gm_803DBF78 to influence codegen.
Used float constants 185.0f and 200.0f for fog animation frame thresholds in fn_801A7FB4.
Represented static format strings inside a struct adjacent to float data in gm_803DBF78, then accessed them by byte offsets 0x24 and 0x30 for sprintf calls.
Used a raw hardware address dereference (*(u32*) 0x800000F8 / 4 / 1000) in the performance overlay conversion path.
Kept a potentially matching-sensitive assignment sequence in fn_801A9FCC where lines[2].next is first set to &lines[3] and then overwritten with NULL, as shown in the diff.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the provided context.
The PR body states this was split from #2345 and limited to game-loop and menu 100% matches.
The PR body notes fn_8023DAEC in mnnamenew.c was dropped because it was already upstream.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or further type fn_80165E7C, fn_8016F870, fn_801A7FB4, fn_801A9FCC, or mn_802308F0.
Check whether struct mn_802307F8_t fields x0, x1, x2, xA, and text were later given semantic names.
Search future gmopening changes for corrections to the PerfLabelLine next-chain behavior in fn_801A9FCC.
Find PRs that further document lbl_803D5A4C_t bonus table semantics and the 0x29A sentinel.
Search for more matches using 0x800000F8 bus-clock conversion patterns.
Check whether fn_801A7FB4's repeated gm_801A4310 calls and entity-count thresholds were later clarified by cutscene/opening-system work.
